title: Multi-Environment Tool Plugin for Hermes Agent

core_idea.summary: Plugin registers 3 tools (env_connect, env_list, env_tool) that let the agent work with N execution environments simultaneously. env_tool is a meta-dispatcher: takes env_slug + tool_name + args, routes to the named environment using existing backend classes. Schema grows by 3 tools total, never more.
ikr: Agent executes commands on local + remote SSH + Docker container in one turn
Adding a new environment = one env_connect call, after which all core tool operations are available for that env
Tool schema does not grow with number of environments — 3 tools regardless of N

env_connect.purpose: Create a named connection to an execution environment

schema_concept.slug: string — user-defined name for this environment (optional, auto-generated if omitted)
schema_concept.type: enum: docker | ssh (local is handled by core Hermes TERMINAL_ENV=local)
schema_concept.timeout: int — command timeout in seconds (optional, default 180)

verifiable_assertions.claim: SSHEnvironment constructor accepts host, user, port, key_path, cwd, timeout as explicit parameters
verifiable_assertions.source: tools/environments/ssh.py:45 — __init__(self, host, user, cwd, timeout, port, key_path)

verifiable_assertions.claim: DockerEnvironment constructor accepts image, cwd, timeout, cpu, memory, disk, persistent_filesystem, task_id, volumes, host_cwd, auto_mount_cwd, forward_env, env, run_as_host_user, extra_args, persist_across_processes
verifiable_assertions.source: tools/terminal_tool.py:1268 — _DockerEnvironment(image=, cwd=, timeout=, cpu=, memory=, disk=, persistent_filesystem=, task_id=, volumes=, host_cwd=, auto_mount_cwd=, forward_env=, env=, run_as_host_user=, extra_args=, persist_across_processes=)

verifiable_assertions.claim: LocalEnvironment exists in core but is NOT used by plugin — local is handled by core Hermes (TERMINAL_ENV=local). Plugin only imports SSHEnvironment and DockerEnvironment.
verifiable_assertions.source: tools/terminal_tool.py:1258 — _LocalEnvironment(cwd=cwd, timeout=timeout) exists but plugin does not import it

verifiable_assertions.claim: All backends implement BaseEnvironment ABC with execute(command, cwd, stdin_data) → {output, returncode}
verifiable_assertions.source: tools/environments/base.py:829 — def execute(self, command, cwd, *, timeout, stdin_data, rewrite_compound_background) → dict

verifiable_assertions.claim: BaseEnvironment.init_session() captures login shell environment snapshot — must be called after construction
SSHEnvironment auto-syncs ~/.hermes/ internals (credentials, skills, cache) on construction via FileSyncManager
SSHEnvironment uses ControlMaster for connection persistence (ControlPersist=300)

env_list.purpose: List all active environment connections with their metadata
env_list.returns: JSON array of {slug, type, status, cwd, connected_at}

verifiable_assertions.claim: Plugin can maintain its own registry dict without touching core _active_environments
verifiable_assertions.source: hermes_cli/plugins.py:315 — PluginContext class gives plugins isolated state; no restriction on module-level mutable state

env_tool.purpose: Execute a core tool operation on a named environment

schema_concept.env_slug: string — target environment identifier from env_connect or preconfigured inventory
schema_concept.tool_name: string — name of the logical operation: terminal | read_file | write_file | patch | search_files | execute_code
schema_concept.args: object — same parameters as the named tool's own core schema (model derives structure from core tool schemas already in system prompt)

verifiable_assertions.claim: ShellFileOperations can be constructed with any BaseEnvironment instance and provides read_file, write_file, patch, search_files
verifiable_assertions.source: tools/file_operations.py:772 — class ShellFileOperations.__init__(self, terminal_env, cwd) where terminal_env is 'Any object with execute(command, cwd) method'

verifiable_assertions.claim: ShellFileOperations._exec() prefers live terminal_env.cwd over init-time cwd — tracks cd commands run via terminal
verifiable_assertions.source: tools/file_operations.py:807 — _exec() cwd resolution order: explicit arg → live self.env.cwd → init-time self.cwd

verifiable_assertions.claim: BaseEnvironment.execute() handles session snapshot sourcing, CWD tracking, interrupt handling, timeout enforcement — shared across all backends
verifiable_assertions.source: tools/environments/base.py:829 — def execute(self, command, cwd, *, timeout, stdin_data, rewrite_compound_background)

verifiable_assertions.claim: env_tool does NOT call core tool handlers — it calls env.execute() and ShellFileOperations directly, bypassing _active_environments entirely
verifiable_assertions.source: design: plugin handler uses self.registry[slug].env.execute() and self.registry[slug].file_ops.read_file() — no reference to tools.terminal_tool._active_environments
verifiable_assertions.verification_method: code inspection of proposed plugin handler — by construction, not core code

verifiable_assertions.claim: Plugin tools receive task_id kwarg via registry.dispatch
verifiable_assertions.source: tools/registry.py:390 — dispatch(self, name, args, **kwargs) → entry.handler(args, **kwargs); model_tools.py:1148 — registry.dispatch(function_name, next_args, task_id=task_id, session_id=session_id)

verifiable_assertions.claim: execute_code core handler hardcodes _get_or_create_env(task_id) which looks up _active_environments[task_id] — cannot be redirected to plugin env without core changes
verifiable_assertions.source: tools/code_execution_tool.py:607-608 — from tools.terminal_tool import _active_environments, _env_lock, _create_environment; line 618: if effective_task_id in _active_environments

verifiable_assertions.claim: Core execute_code cannot be reused for plugin envs without core changes — _get_or_create_env(task_id) hardcodes _active_environments lookup. Plugin provides two alternatives: Path A (plain Python, MVP) and Path C (custom RPC poll loop with plugin-side dispatch, full tool-RPC parity).
verifiable_assertions.source: tools/code_execution_tool.py:607-608,618,635 — hardcoded global lookup; Path C reuses generate_hermes_tools_module + _ship_file_to_remote + _env_temp_dir (all public) and replaces only the dispatch layer
verifiable_assertions.verification_method: source code inspection + logical deduction from core coupling constraint

state.file_ops: dict[str, ShellFileOperations] — slug → file ops wrapper
state.meta: dict[str, dict] — slug → {type, status, cwd, connected_at, sync_policy}

lifecycle: env_connect → create env instance via imported backend class → env.init_session() → store in registry → return slug
env_tool → lookup registry[slug] → dispatch to env.execute() or file_ops method → return result
cleanup on session_end hook → iterate registry → env.cleanup() for each

no_core_modification.claim: Plugin imports backend classes and ShellFileOperations but does not modify any core file

verifiable_assertion.claim: Plugins MUST NOT modify core files — this is a firm constraint
verifiable_assertion.source: AGENTS.md — 'plugins MUST NOT modify core files (run_agent.py, cli.py, gateway/run.py, hermes_cli/main.py, etc.)'

no_active_environments_mutation.claim: Plugin never reads or writes tools.terminal_tool._active_environments
no_active_environments_mutation.rationale: Avoids race conditions with concurrent sessions, subagents, and core cleanup thread

verifiable_assertion.claim: _active_environments is a process-global dict protected by _env_lock — mutating it from plugin risks cross-session bleed
verifiable_assertion.source: tools/terminal_tool.py — _active_environments: Dict, _env_lock: threading.Lock, _start_cleanup_thread() reaps idle envs

why_three_tools_not_one.rationale: env_connect / env_list / env_tool separates connection management, introspection, and dispatch — cleaner than single mega-tool with action enum
why_three_tools_not_one.benefit: Adding new core tool support requires zero schema changes — env_tool.tool_name is a string, not an enum

why_env_tool_is_meta_dispatcher.rationale: Instead of N×M tools (ssh_terminal, docker_terminal, ssh_read_file, ...), one dispatcher routes (env_slug, tool_name) to the right env + operation
why_env_tool_is_meta_dispatcher.schema_impact: 3 tools total. Δ per new environment = 0. Δ per new supported tool = 0 (just add to internal dispatch table, no schema change).

verifiable_assertion.claim: Every tool in schema costs on every API call — footprint rule
verifiable_assertion.source: AGENTS.md — 'Every model tool we add is sent on every API call, so the bar for a new core tool is high'

why_not_context_switch.rationale: Swapping _active_environments['default'] is race-prone with concurrent sessions and breaks execute_code backend type detection

verifiable_assertion.claim: execute_code reads _get_env_config()['env_type'] which returns TERMINAL_ENV — if switched env type differs from configured, behavior is wrong

why_not_target_parameter_on_core_tools.rationale: Core tool schemas do not include 'target' parameter. Adding it requires modifying core schema definitions — violates plugin-only constraint

verifiable_assertion.claim: Core tool schemas are defined in tools/*.py at registry.register() time — plugin cannot modify them without core changes
verifiable_assertion.source: tools/registry.py:234 — registry.register(name, toolset, schema, ...) — schema is passed at registration, immutable after

sync_strategy.decision: Per-env sync_policy set at connect time

options.none: No sync — agent works with remote FS directly via shell commands (suitable for untrusted hosts, Docker bind-mounts)
options.minimal_hermes: Sync ~/.hermes/credentials + ~/.hermes/skills + ~/.hermes/cache only (SSHEnvironment does this automatically via FileSyncManager)
options.full_hermes: Full ~/.hermes/ sync including sync_back on disconnect

sync_strategy.default: minimal_hermes for SSH, none for Docker/local

verifiable_assertion.claim: FileSyncManager syncs only ~/.hermes/ internals (credentials, skills, cache) — NOT user project files
verifiable_assertion.source: tools/environments/file_sync.py:50-76 — iter_sync_files() combines get_credential_file_mounts() + iter_skills_files() + iter_cache_files()

verifiable_assertion.claim: User project files on SSH backend are manipulated directly on remote via shell commands (cat, sed, find, grep) — not synced
verifiable_assertion.source: skills/autonomous-ai-agents/hermes-agent/SKILL.md:1063 — 'when TERMINAL_ENV != local, every file tool runs inside the backend container, not on the host'

execute_code_strategy.decision: Two-tier: MVP = Path A (plain Python), upgrade = Path C (custom RPC poll loop with plugin-side dispatch)

paths_evaluated.name: Plain Python (no tool-RPC)
paths_evaluated.mechanism: Write script.py to remote via env.execute('echo | base64 -d'), run via env.execute('python3 script.py'), return stdout

paths_evaluated.name: Full RPC, tools route to DEFAULT env (broken)
paths_evaluated.mechanism: Copy _execute_remote() + _rpc_poll_loop(), but poll loop calls handle_function_call(tool, args, task_id) → core dispatcher → _active_environments[task_id] → DEFAULT env
paths_evaluated.verdict: REJECTED — tool calls from script on serverA would execute on DEFAULT env (local), not on serverA. Nonsensical.

paths_evaluated.name: Full RPC, custom poll loop with plugin dispatch (CORRECT)
paths_evaluated.mechanism: Copy _execute_remote() + _rpc_poll_loop() logic, replace handle_function_call with plugin-side dispatch table that routes tool calls to the SAME env the script runs on
paths_evaluated.loc_estimate: ~150 lines (mostly copied from core, adapted dispatch)
paths_evaluated.verdict: RECOMMENDED for full parity — ship after MVP

paths_evaluated.name: Monkeypatch _active_environments[task_id]
paths_evaluated.mechanism: Inject plugin env into _active_environments['multienv_slug'], call core _execute_remote(code, task_id='multienv_slug'), restore after
paths_evaluated.verdict: REJECTED — race-prone, _get_env_config() returns wrong env_type, concurrency issues with subagents

path_c_detail.summary: Plugin reimplements the remote execute_code flow with a custom RPC poll loop. Script runs on plugin env (e.g. serverA). Tool calls from inside the script are dispatched by the plugin directly to the same env's execute() and ShellFileOperations — NOT through core handle_function_call().
data_flow: 3. Plugin: generate hermes_tools.py via imported generate_hermes_tools_module(enabled_tools, transport='file')
4. Plugin: ship hermes_tools.py + script.py to remote via _ship_file_to_remote(env, remote_path, content)
5. Plugin: create sandbox dir + rpc dir on remote via env.execute('mkdir -p ...')
6. Plugin: start _plugin_rpc_poll_loop thread — polls remote rpc dir for req_* files
7. Plugin: env.execute('cd sandbox && HERMES_RPC_DIR=... python3 script.py') — script runs on remote
8. Script imports hermes_tools, calls terminal('ls') → writes req_000001 file on remote
9. Plugin poll loop: env.execute('cat req_000001') → reads request → PLUGIN DISPATCH (not handle_function_call)
10. Plugin dispatch: terminal → env.execute(args['command']), read_file → file_ops.read_file(args['path']), etc.
11. Plugin poll loop: env.execute('echo ... | base64 -d > res_000001') → writes response on remote
13. Script finishes → env.execute returns stdout → plugin returns to model
14. Cleanup: stop poll thread, env.execute('rm -rf sandbox_dir')

plugin_dispatch_table.search_files: file_ops.search(args['pattern'], path=args.get('path','.'), target=args.get('target','content'), file_glob=args.get('file_glob'), limit=args.get('limit',50), output_mode=args.get('output_mode','content'), context=args.get('context',0))
plugin_dispatch_table.patch: file_ops.patch_replace(args['path'], args['old_string'], args['new_string'], replace_all=args.get('replace_all',False)) if args.get('mode','replace')=='replace' else file_ops.patch_v4a(args['patch'])
plugin_dispatch_table.web_search: handle_function_call('web_search', args, task_id=task_id) — NOT env-coupled, safe to use core dispatcher
plugin_dispatch_table.web_extract: handle_function_call('web_extract', args, task_id=task_id) — NOT env-coupled, safe to use core dispatcher

key_functions_imported_from_core.purpose: Generate hermes_tools.py stub module with file-based RPC functions for 7 sandbox-allowed tools
Write content to remote path via base64-encoded echo pipe — works on all backends including Modal
Return writable temp dir for the env (uses env.get_temp_dir() or falls back to /tmp)
Frozenset of 7 tools allowed inside sandbox: web_search, web_extract, read_file, write_file, search_files, patch, terminal
Params to strip from terminal calls inside sandbox: background, pty, notify_on_complete, watch_patterns

what_plugin_copies_and_adapts.adaptation: Replace _get_or_create_env(task_id) with registry[slug].env. Replace _rpc_poll_loop call with _plugin_rpc_poll_loop. Keep: sandbox dir creation, file shipping, script execution, cleanup, output post-processing (truncation, ANSI strip, redaction).

Replace handle_function_call(tool_name, tool_args, task_id=task_id) with plugin dispatch table. Keep: poll interval, req file listing, atomic read, response writing via base64, req file cleanup, tool call counter, tool call log, stop_event handling. Add file_ops parameter.

what_plugin_does_NOT_copy: Output post-processing — can import strip_ansi and redact_sensitive_text directly
UDS transport (local backend) — not needed, plugin envs always use file-based RPC

web_search_handling.note: web_search and web_extract are NOT env-coupled — they make HTTP calls from the host process. Plugin dispatch routes them through core handle_function_call() safely.

verifiable_assertion.claim: web_search and web_extract handlers do not reference _active_environments or terminal env
verifiable_assertion.source: SANDBOX_ALLOWED_TOOLS — only terminal, read_file, write_file, search_files, patch are env-coupled. web_search/web_extract make HTTP calls from host.
verifiable_assertion.verification_method: code inspection — web_search/web_extract tools do not import terminal_tool or _active_environments

local_env_special_case.note: For env_type='local', Path C uses file-based RPC (not UDS). Slightly less efficient than core's UDS path but avoids _active_environments coupling. Script runs via env.execute('python3 script.py') which is subprocess.Popen.
local_env_special_case.tradeoff: File-based RPC adds ~50ms latency per tool call vs UDS. Acceptable for plugin.

execute_code_strategy.rationale: Core execute_code couples backend type to global config (_get_env_config), not to env instance. Cannot redirect without core changes. Path C reuses core's RPC protocol and file-generation logic while replacing only the dispatch layer.

verifiable_assertion.claim: Core execute_code calls _get_or_create_env(task_id) which uses _active_environments and _get_env_config — hardcoded global lookup
generate_hermes_tools_module is a public function — can be imported and called by plugin
_ship_file_to_remote is a public function — works on all backends
File-based RPC protocol is defined entirely in generated hermes_tools.py stub — no hidden state
verifiable_assertion.source: tools/code_execution_tool.py:400-457 — _FILE_TRANSPORT_HEADER defines req_NNNNNN / res_NNNNNN file protocol

verifiable_assertion.claim: 5 of 7 sandbox tools are env-coupled (terminal, read_file, write_file, search_files, patch). 2 are not (web_search, web_extract).

execute_code_strategy.upgrade_path: If core maintainers extract execute_code env-selection into injectable parameter, plugin can switch from Path C to calling core _execute_remote directly with plugin env. Until then: Path C for full parity, Path A for MVP.

cleanup_strategy.decision: on_session_end lifecycle hook + explicit env_disconnect (if added as 4th tool) + BaseEnvironment.__del__ fallback

verifiable_assertion.claim: Plugin lifecycle hooks include on_session_end — called when agent session terminates
verifiable_assertion.source: hermes_cli/plugins.py:22-26 — VALID_HOOKS includes on_session_end; model_tools.py and run_agent.py invoke hooks

verifiable_assertion.claim: BaseEnvironment.__del__ calls cleanup() as fallback — SSHEnvironment.cleanup() does sync_back + closes ControlMaster socket
verifiable_assertion.source: tools/environments/base.py:885 — def __del__(self): self.cleanup(); tools/environments/ssh.py:355 — def cleanup(self): sync_manager.sync_back(); close control socket

default_tools_unaffected.claim: Default terminal, read_file, write_file, patch, search_files, execute_code continue working through TERMINAL_ENV as before

verifiable_assertion.source: Plugin does not touch _active_environments, does not override core tools (no override=True), registers separate toolset 'multienv'
verifiable_assertion.verification_method: by construction — plugin operates in isolated namespace

typical_ssh_docker_local.description: Agent runs ls on serverA (SSH), reads file from serverA, runs ls locally (core tools)
typical_ssh_docker_local.precondition: serverA preconfigured in plugin inventory, TERMINAL_ENV=local for core tools
typical_ssh_docker_local.note: 3rd call uses core terminal (TERMINAL_ENV=local), not env_tool

first_connect.description: Agent connects to a new server, then runs a command and reads a file

mixed_core_and_plugin.description: Agent writes file locally (core), runs command on remote (plugin), patches file on remote (plugin)

R1_N_environments_simultaneously.mechanism: EnvironmentRegistry holds N envs simultaneously, env_tool dispatches by slug

R2_full_toolset_per_env.mechanism: env_tool supports terminal, read_file, write_file, patch, search_files, execute_code per env
R2_full_toolset_per_env.caveat: MVP: execute_code = plain Python (Path A). Upgrade: full tool-RPC parity via Path C (custom RPC poll loop with plugin-side dispatch to env.execute + ShellFileOperations).

R3_schema_does_not_grow.mechanism: 3 tools total. Δ per env = 0. Δ per new supported tool = 0 (internal dispatch table, no schema change).

R4_reuse_backend_classes.mechanism: Plugin imports SSHEnvironment, DockerEnvironment, ShellFileOperations directly. LocalEnvironment NOT imported — local handled by core.

R5_no_core_modification.mechanism: Plugin-only. No override=True on core tools. No mutation of _active_environments.

R6_default_tools_unaffected.mechanism: Plugin registers separate 'multienv' toolset. Core tools continue through TERMINAL_ENV.

R7_connection_efficiency.mechanism: SSHEnvironment uses ControlMaster (ControlPersist=300). DockerEnvironment uses persistent container. Env instances cached in registry.

R8_minimal_model_overhead.mechanism: Typical task = 3 tool-calls (connect + 2 operations, or 3 operations if preconnected)

open_questions.question: Should env_disconnect be a 4th tool or an action within env_connect (action=disconnect)?
open_questions.current_leaning: 4th tool env_disconnect(slug) — symmetric with env_connect, clear semantics

open_questions.question: Should plugin support preconfigured environment inventory in config.yaml (like ansible inventory) or only ad-hoc env_connect?
open_questions.current_leaning: Support both — config.yaml for persistent envs, env_connect for ad-hoc

open_questions.question: How to handle model confusion between env_tool args nesting and core tool flat params?
open_questions.current_leaning: Clear schema description: 'args: same parameters as the named tool's own schema'. Model derives from core schemas already in system prompt.
open_questions.mitigation: Skill document with examples of env_tool usage

open_questions.question: Should env_tool support process management (background tasks on remote)?
open_questions.current_leaning: MVP: no. Process registry is core-coupled (process_registry.py). Future: env_tool with tool_name='process' if feasible.

source_files_verified: tools/code_execution_tool.py (lines 1-100, 250-460, 600-1100, 1567-1600 — full execute_code mechanism, RPC protocol, Path C source)
